title: System and method of capturing and rendering a stereoscopic panorama using a depth buffer
abstract: A system and method of capturing and rendering stereoscopic panoramic content is provided. The system may capture information from a virtual scene to generate a stereoscopic panorama in the form of a stereoscopic cube map. Using the pre-captured cube map, the system may render stereoscopic images during gameplay, image viewing, and/or playback of a three dimensional video. The system may capture camera distance information during capture for use in distortion reduction during image rendering and playback. The system may implement additional distortion reduction measures. Further, the system may use the capture camera distance information to write to depth buffers corresponding to rendered images for use when introducing dynamic gameplay elements into the scene. Additionally, the system may use realistic camera models to simulate human anatomy and reduce distortions associated with simplistic camera models.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A computer implemented method of rendering stereoscopic images captured from virtual scene data for virtual reality display, the method being implemented in a computer system having one or more physical processors programmed with computer program instructions that, when executed by the one or more physical processors, cause the computer system to perform the method, the method comprising: obtaining, by the computer system, a stereoscopic panorama data set representative of virtual scene data, the stereoscopic panorama data set including left camera visual data, left camera distance data, right camera visual data, and right camera distance data; establishing, by the computer system, a camera model having a left camera corresponding to a left eye of a user and a right camera corresponding to a right eye of the user; determining, by the computer system, a location, and a viewing angle of the camera model; rendering, by the computer system, a left eye image according to the left camera visual data and the left camera distance data, based on the location, and the viewing angle of the camera model; rendering, by the computer system, a right eye image according to the right camera visual data and the right camera distance data, based on the location, and the viewing angle of the camera model; and causing, by the computer system, the display of the right eye image and the left eye image to produce a stereoscopic effect. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein rendering the right eye image further includes generating, by the computer system, a right camera depth buffer including depth information of a plurality of graphic units of the right eye image, and wherein rendering the left eye image further includes generating, by the computer system, a left camera depth buffer including depth information of a plurality of graphic units of the left eye image. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: obtaining, by the computer system, imaging information of a dynamic scene element, the imaging information including visual information, depth information, and location information; determining, by the computer system, a left eye rendering location in the left eye image and a right eye rendering location in the right eye image of the dynamic scene element; comparing, by the computer system, depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the left eye rendering location; comparing, by the computer system, depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the right eye rendering location; determining, by the computer system, to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the left eye rendering location; determining, by the computer system, to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the right eye rendering location. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein rendering the right eye image includes rendering the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image; and wherein rendering the left eye image includes rendering the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein determining to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image includes determining to render a first portion of the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the right eye rendering location, 

 wherein determining to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image includes determining to render a second portion of the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the left eye rendering location, wherein rendering the right eye image includes rendering the first portion of the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image; and wherein rendering the left eye image includes rendering the second portion of the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image. 6. The method of claim 3, further comprising: obtaining, by the computer system, second location imaging information of a dynamic scene element, the second location imaging information including second location visual information, second location depth information, and second location information; determining, by the computer system, a second left eye rendering location in the left eye image and a second right eye rendering location in the right eye image of the dynamic scene element; comparing, by the computer system, second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the second left eye rendering location; comparing, by the computer system, second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the second right eye rendering location; determining, by the computer system, to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the second left eye rendering location based on the comparison between the second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the second left eye rendering location; determining, by the computer system, to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the second right eye rendering location based on the comparison between the second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the second right eye rendering location. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein rendering the left eye image includes rendering the left eye image based on a downward viewing angle of the camera model, and rendering the right eye image includes rendering the right eye image based on a downward viewing angle of the camera model, the method further comprising: obtaining, by the computer system, a distortion correction image including visual data and camera distance data captured from the virtual scene in a downward direction from a virtual user location; comparing, by the computer system, the distortion correction image visual data to the left eye image; comparing, by the computer system, the distortion correction image visual data to the right eye image; rerendering, by the computer system, a portion of the left eye image with a portion of the distortion correction image visual data based on the comparison between the distortion correction image visual data and the left eye image; and rerendering, by the computer system, a portion of the right eye image with a portion of the distortion correction image visual data based on the comparison between the distortion correction image visual data and the right eye image. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the precision of individual values of the left camera distance data and the right camera distance data is determined according to magnitude of the individual values. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein an axis of rotation for determining the viewing angle of the camera model is located behind and equidistant from the left camera and the right camera. 10. A system of rendering stereoscopic images captured from virtual scene data for virtual reality display, the system comprising: a computer system comprising one or more physical processors programmed by computer program instructions that, when executed, cause the computer system to: 

 obtain a stereoscopic panorama data set representative of the virtual scene data, the stereoscopic panorama data set including left camera visual data, left camera distance data, right camera visual data, and right camera distance data; establish a camera model having a left camera corresponding to a left eye of a user and a right camera corresponding to a right eye of the user; determine a location, and a viewing angle of the camera model; render a left eye image according to the left camera visual data and the left camera distance data, based on the location, and the viewing angle of the camera model; render a right eye image according to the right camera visual data and the right camera distance data, based on the location, and the viewing angle of the camera model; and cause the display of the right eye image and the left eye image to produce a stereoscopic effect. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the computer system is further programmed to render the right eye image by generating a right camera depth buffer including depth information of a plurality of graphic units of the right eye image, and wherein the computer system is further programmed render the left eye image by generating a left camera depth buffer including depth information of a plurality of graphic units of the left eye image. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the computer system is further programmed to: obtain imaging information of a dynamic scene element, the imaging information including visual information, depth information, and location information; determine a left eye rendering location in the left eye image and a right eye rendering location in the right eye image of the dynamic scene element; compare depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the left eye rendering location; compare depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the right eye rendering location; 

 decide whether to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the left eye rendering location; decide whether to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the right eye rendering location. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the computer system is further programmed to render the right eye image by rendering the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image; and wherein the computer system is further programmed to render the left eye image by rendering the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the computer system is further programmed to decide to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image by determining whether to render a right-eye portion of the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the right eye rendering location, wherein the computer system is further programmed to decide to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image by determining whether to render a left-eye portion of the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location based on the comparison between the depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the left eye rendering location, wherein the computer system is further programmed to render the right eye image by rendering the right-eye portion of the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the right eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image; and 

 wherein the computer system is further programmed to render the left eye image by rendering the left-eye portion of the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the left eye rendering location according to the decision to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image. 15. The system of claim 13, wherein the computer system is further programmed to: obtain second location imaging information of a dynamic scene element, the second location imaging information including second location visual information, second location depth information, and second location information; determine a second left eye rendering location in the left eye image and a second right eye rendering location in the right eye image of the dynamic scene element; compare second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the second left eye rendering location; compare second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the second right eye rendering location; decide whether to render the dynamic scene element in the left eye image at the second left eye rendering location based on the comparison between the second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the left camera depth buffer at the second left eye rendering location; decide whether to render the dynamic scene element in the right eye image at the second right eye rendering location based on the comparison between the second location depth information of the dynamic scene element to the right camera depth buffer at the second right eye rendering location. 16. The system of claim 10, wherein the computer system is further programmed to render the left eye image by rendering the left eye image based on a downward viewing angle of the camera model, and render the right eye image by rendering the right eye image based on a downward viewing angle of the camera model; and the computer system is further programmed to: obtain a distortion correction image including visual data and camera distance data captured from the virtual scene in a downward direction from a virtual user location; 

 compare the distortion correction image visual data to the left eye image; compare the distortion correction image visual data to the right eye image; rerender a portion of the left eye image with a portion of the distortion correction image visual data based on the comparison between the distortion correction image visual data and the left eye image; and rerender a portion of the right eye image with a portion of the distortion correction image visual data based on the comparison between the distortion correction image visual data and the right eye image. 17. The system of claim 10, wherein the precision of individual values of the left camera distance data and the right camera distance data is set according to magnitude of the individual values. 18. The system of claim 10, wherein an axis of rotation for determining the viewing angle of the camera model is located behind and equidistant from the left camera and the right camera. 19. A system of capturing stereoscopic images captured from virtual scene data for virtual reality display, the system comprising: a computer system comprising one or more physical processors programmed by computer program instructions that, when executed, cause the computer system to: obtain virtual scene data; establish a capture camera model having: a left camera corresponding to a left eye of a user, a right camera corresponding to a right eye of the user, a vertical axis of rotation located behind a camera plane, the camera plane intersecting with the left camera and the right camera, and the vertical axis of rotation equidistant to the left camera and the right camera, and a horizontal axis of rotation, 

 determine a first orientation of the capture camera model, the first orientation specified by a first horizontal angle of the camera plane around the vertical axis of rotation and a first vertical angle of the camera plane around the horizontal axis of rotation; capture, in the first orientation, first left camera visual data, first left camera distance data, first right camera visual data, and first right camera distance data from the virtual scene data for a stereoscopic panorama data set; determine a second orientation of the capture camera model, the second orientation specified by a second horizontal angle of the camera plane around the vertical axis of rotation and a second vertical angle of the camera plane around the horizontal axis of rotation; and capture, in the second orientation, second left camera visual data, second left camera distance data, second right camera visual data, and second right camera distance data from the virtual scene data for a stereoscopic panorama data set.
